description: End-to-end integration coverage for unified cloud lifecycle commands, workflow-kind persistence, artifacts, redaction, and CI gating.

userStories.title: Map integration test entrypoints and lifecycle command surface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clear map of integration test package entrypoints and cloud lifecycle command handlers so that new end-to-end tests are added in the correct location with the correct build tags.
acceptanceCriteria: A single integration test scaffold file identifies the target package/path and required integration build tag for the lifecycle suite.
The lifecycle command sequence (setup, run, auto, review, status, logs, pull, cancel) is enumerated as test constants or table entries for reuse.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create shared lifecycle fixtures and contract key definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared fixture definitions for workflow states, workflow kinds, artifact groups, and expected response keys so that all scenarios assert against one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared fixture definitions exist for lifecycle checkpoints used by setup/run/status/logs/pull/cancel scenarios.
Expected JSON response keys are defined in camelCase constants for test assertions.
Fixture definitions include workflowKind expectations for run, auto, and review.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ephemeral cloud integration harness
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an isolated harness that provisions temporary workspace, store, and runtime dependencies so that lifecycle integration tests run deterministically without external services.
acceptanceCriteria: Harness creates a unique temp directory and isolated state per test invocation.
Harness wires test-double or ephemeral store/runtime factories with no external network dependency.
Harness exposes setup and teardown helpers used by integration scenarios.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add reusable cloud command execution helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable command runner with injectable stdin/stdout so that lifecycle scenarios can execute cloud-capable commands consistently in tests.
acceptanceCriteria: A helper executes target commands with configurable args and injected input/output streams.
Helper returns captured human-readable output and command errors for assertions.
Helper supports running the same command in --json mode within the same test flow.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add JSON decode and deterministic normalization utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared output utilities that decode JSON and normalize nondeterministic fields so that lifecycle assertions stay stable across runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Utility decodes --json command output into assertion-friendly structures.
Utility normalizes only configured nondeterministic fields (for example timestamps or generated IDs).
Normalization is applied through a shared helper used by at least one scenario test.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement baseline lifecycle scenario: setup -> run --cloud -> status
userStories.description: As a user, I need the baseline cloud lifecycle flow to work end-to-end so that setup and cloud run create a workflow visible through status.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test executes hal cloud setup, hal run --cloud, and hal cloud status successfully in one scenario.
Scenario asserts persisted workflow state exists after run and is queryable by status.
Scenario validates both human output and --json output at run and status checkpoints.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add lifecycle logs scenario assertions
userStories.description: As a user, I need cloud logs retrieval to work after workflow execution so that I can inspect workflow output from the lifecycle interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test executes hal cloud logs for a workflow created in the harness.
Logs assertions validate expected contract keys in human and --json outputs.
Scenario verifies logs command references the targeted workflow identifier.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add lifecycle pull scenario with artifact file checks
userStories.description: As a user, I need cloud artifact pull to succeed so that produced files and artifact metadata are available locally after workflow completion.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test executes hal cloud pull for a completed workflow.
Scenario asserts artifact group metadata matches expected workflow-kind context.
Scenario asserts pulled artifact files exist at expected output paths.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement auto --cloud lifecycle scenario
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an auto workflow lifecycle scenario so that unified cloud behavior is validated for autonomous execution paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test executes hal auto --cloud and then queries lifecycle state via status.
Scenario asserts persisted workflowKind equals auto in store-backed state and --json response.
Scenario executes and asserts one downstream command after status (logs or pull).
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement review --cloud lifecycle scenario
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a review workflow lifecycle scenario so that unified cloud behavior is validated for review command execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test executes hal review --cloud and then queries lifecycle state via status.
Scenario asserts persisted workflowKind equals review in store-backed state and --json response.
Scenario validates one additional lifecycle checkpoint beyond status (logs or pull).
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement cancel-path lifecycle scenario
userStories.description: As a user, I need hal cloud cancel to move active workflows into a terminal canceled state so that cancellation behavior is reliable and observable.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test starts a cancellable workflow and executes hal cloud cancel.
Post-cancel status assertion verifies the workflow reaches the expected terminal canceled state.
Scenario validates cancel/status consistency in both human and --json outputs.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce suite-wide redaction and camelCase JSON contract assertions
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I need lifecycle tests to enforce redaction and stable JSON field naming so that cloud/auth outputs remain safe and backward compatible.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared assertions verify secret-bearing values are redacted in human-readable outputs for covered lifecycle commands.
Shared assertions verify secret-bearing values are redacted in --json outputs for covered lifecycle commands.
JSON contract assertions check expected camelCase keys for cloud/auth responses used in the suite.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire lifecycle integration suite into CI and local reproducibility docs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need pull requests to run lifecycle integration tests automatically and document local execution so that regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: CI pull-request workflow runs the cloud lifecycle integration test target.
CI configuration fails the job when lifecycle integration tests fail.
Repository docs or make target instructions include the exact command to run the lifecycle suite locally.
Typecheck passes
